calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Herb Thomas.

The Court noted that this matter was continued at the last hearing for Ms. Hall to address the issue of why the special needs trust should be paying for two trustees. Ms. Hall stated that Mr. Thomas is withdrawing his fee request and that she has nothing to add with regard to her fees. The Second and Final Account, etc. is under submission. The Court will remove from the order anything that has to do with Mr. Thomas' fees and make adjustments if necessary.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Continued To Jul-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Herb Thomas (Trustee).

Ms. Hall stated that the Director of CPT was out of the Country, that she spoke with Mr. Lindall yesterday and that it looks like there was fee schedule that was approved for a flat fee of $1,800.00. Ms. Hall also stated that a portion of that in the amount of $385.00 to $390.00 gets paid to Mr. Thomas when CPT receives the $1,800.00 and that Mr. Thomas then asks for an hourly fee at a reduced rate. The Court stated that if CPT agreed to do the work, they should be paying Mr. Thomas to do the work. Ms. Hall stated that she is working on obtaining a list of cases that involve CPT and requested a continuance to meet with Mr. Thomas. The Court stated that it is very troubled with the idea that Mr. Thomas should get anything out of the trust when he should be getting paid by CPT. The Second and Final Account, etc. is continued to July 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Hall is to file a supplement or an amendment by July 16, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Herb Thomas.

The Court questions why both CPT and Mr. Thomas are being paid as the Trustees. Ms. Hall stated that CPT could not be bonded in California at the time and took on someone that could be, and that CPT handles a portion of the investment. The Court noted that CPT is asking for a 1% fee. Mr. Hall stated that there aren't very many funds left, that they are looking for fees for the Trustees, the Attorney, etc., that none of them are going to be paid and that they have agreed to pro rating the fees. Ms. Hall also stated that they certainly are not going to get all of their fees, that CPT was unbonded the entire time, that she does not have time records for CPT, and that CPT typically does not provide time records, but she can reach out to them. The Court expressed concern about paying an unbonded entity. The Second and Final Account, etc. is continued to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any supplements are to be filed by June 20, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-26-2018 Continued To Jan-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mercy Hall, for petitioner Charities Pooled Trust.

Ms. Hall reported that the accounting had been submitted to efiling last week, and that she had not been informed it had been rejected. The Court noted that it did not as yet appear in the Register of Actions, and the matter was continued to January 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-28-2018 Continued To Dec-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Herb Thomas (Trustee).
Bruce Feder Jewish Family and Children's Services (Conservator).

The Court provided copies of two pleadings filed by Mr. Feder's office reflecting that he is the Attorney of record for CPT (Trustee). Ms. Feder stated that shortly after those filings he was told that Ms. Hall was going to be taking over and that he did not verify whether or not a substitution of attorney was filed. Ms. Hall explained that Mr. Feder's office filed the first accounting for CPT, that at that time Mr. Thomas was brought in as Directed Trustee because CPT could not be bonded, that she represents Herb Thomas, and that there is a Trustee and a Directed Trustee. Ms. Hall also stated that Mr. Thomas is the one who will be filing the accountings and that there has been some confusion with the e-filing clerks because of how the Court's records reflect who the attorneys and who the Trustees are. Mr. Feder stated that is seems Mr. Thomas has been authorized/designated by CPT to file on their behalf, that CPT holds the assets and that he does not believe CPT will be making any appearances. Ms. Hall stated that the accounting was re-filed this morning with an explanation to the E-File Clerk, that the master trust agreement which governs the pooled trust listed Mr. Thomas as Directed Trustee and that she will file a declaration indicating who represents who and what their rolls are. Status on filing of the second account is continued to December 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Continued To Nov-28-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family & Children's Services (Conservator) and spedially appearing for Attorney Mercy Hall on behalf of the Trustee.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that she represents the Conservator, that Ms. Hall represents the Trustee and that the Court's records showing her firm as representing the Trustee is a mistake on the Court's part. The Clerk explained that the declaration filed by Mr. Feder shows that he represents Charities Pooled Trust, that this is where the Filing Clerks obtained the information that was entered on the Court's records and that this is what may have caused the confusion. Ms. Friedeberg stated that Ms. Hall will be making her appearance and that Ms. Hall is requesting a two week continuance. The Court noted that Ms. Hall has not appeared at the last two hearings. Status on filing of the second account is continued to November 28, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-29-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Bruce Feder to appear on November 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the Accounting Petition has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Continued To Oct-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Sheila Robello specially appearing for Mercy Hall.

Ms. Robello stated that Ms. Hall is in the process of getting the accounting filed with the hope of having the same hearing date as the Conservatorship matter. The status on filing the 2nd accounting is continued to October 29, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Oct-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall for Herb Thomas.

Ms. Hall stated that she received the accounting last night, that she will have it filed within the next two weeks, and that she believes the Conservator and her Attorney are filing their petition for fees within the next two weeks. Status on filing of the second account is continued to October 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
